fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Oregon CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Oregon - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity oregon, how to transfer CNA license to oregon, oregon CNA endorsement application

board.name: Oregon State Board of Nursing
board.mailing_address: 17938 SW Upper Boones Ferry Road, Portland OR 97224

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Oregon.

steps.title: Create Account & Submit Application
steps.description: Register and create an account on the OSBN Nurse Portal. Once logged in, submit the online application for CNA certification by examination or endorsement.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Pay the required application fees securely through the OSBN Nurse Portal as part of your application submission.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by the OSBN to complete a criminal background check, which includes fingerprinting.

steps.title: Pass NNAAP Exam (Certification by Examination Only)
steps.description: If applying for certification by examination, register with Credentia and successfully pass the National Nurse Aide Assessment Program (NNAAP) exam.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Ensure all necessary supporting documents, such as training verification or out-of-state license verification (for endorsement), are submitted as instructed by the OSBN.

steps.title: Receive Certification Decision
steps.description: Once all requirements are met and verified, the OSBN will issue your CNA certification, which can be viewed and verified through the OSBN Nurse Portal.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Oregon CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $60. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Oregon.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Oregon?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Oregon while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in OR.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Oregon.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Oregon
hero.dek: To apply for a Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) license in Oregon, you must register and submit your application through the Oregon State Board of Nursing (OSBN) Nurse Portal.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Oregon
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Oregon CNA certification.

official_links.registration_steps: Applicants must create an account on the OSBN Nurse Portal by clicking 'Register' and following the prompts to set up a username and password before starting an application.
